task_completion.description: Open the dwanko_run.tnz scene in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open, and the title bar, scene browser, or xsheet shows 'dwanko_run.tnz' loaded.

task_completion.description: Apply a color inversion (negative) effect to the animation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX schematic or timeline shows an 'Invert' or 'Negative' effect node applied to the scene, OR the main viewer shows the character and background with inverted colors (e.g., a dark background instead of a light one).

task_completion.description: Set the output format to a PNG sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window is open with 'png' selected in the file format dropdown.
The Output Settings window shows the 'Save In' or output path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/negative_render/'.

task_completion.description: Render the animation to generate the PNG sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar is visible in OpenToonz, OR a file manager/terminal shows multiple PNG files (at least 10) generated in the 'negative_render' directory with visually inverted colors.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to apply the effect and render the sequence.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenToonz interface (FX schematic, Output Settings, Render dialog) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts or tools (like ImageMagick or Python) to invert the colors.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows running commands like 'convert -negate' or Python scripts are visible for processing the images outside of OpenToonz.

integrity.description: The agent actually rendered the sequence from OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz render progress window is seen, or the output settings match the generated files, rather than files being manually copied or moved from a pre-existing completed folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Source scene: /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. Output directory: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/negative_render. The output must be a PNG sequence with at least 10 frames. The color inversion effect should result in a dark background (maximum background brightness of 50).
